research_gaps: No formal security audit reports found from recognised auditing firms.
No Allora-specific models or datasets on HuggingFace.
Not listed on DeFiLlama — no independent TVL or protocol revenue data.
Exact X/Twitter follower count not confirmed.
Real mainnet-only inference volume not publicly separated from testnet figures.
Current validator count may have expanded beyond the initial 16 at launch.
Governance participation rate and proposal count unknown.
Total supply discrepancy: official blog says 1B max, ICO Drops reports 786.5M total supply.
No independent verification of 692M inferences claim — self-reported only.

identity.name: Allora Network
identity.one_liner: Decentralised prediction and inference network that aggregates competing ML models to produce collective intelligence, built on Cosmos SDK.

founding_team.name: Nick Emmons
founding_team.background: Co-founded Upshot in 2019 (NFT appraisal). Pivoted to Allora Labs February 2024.

founding_team.name: Kenny Peluso
founding_team.background: BSc Applied Mathematics, Brown University. Full-stack developer at John Hancock before co-founding Upshot/Allora.

team.entity_jurisdiction: Cayman Islands (registered), operations in New York/Boston
team.pivot_history: Originally founded as Upshot (2019) for NFT appraisal using peer prediction. Rebranded to Allora Labs February 2024, pivoting to decentralised AI inference.

technical.architecture_summary: L1 blockchain on Cosmos SDK with CometBFT consensus. Network organised around Topics — sub-networks for specific prediction problems. Workers generate inferences, Forecast Workers predict accuracy, Reputers evaluate against ground truth, Validators secure chain. Inference Synthesis aggregates weighted predictions using regret-based system. Consumer contracts on Arbitrum One for cross-chain inference consumption.
technical.consensus_mechanism: CometBFT DPoS. 25% of emissions to validators, 75% to intelligence layer (workers, forecasters, reputers).
technical.what_is_onchain: Topic creation, worker/reputer registration, staking, reward distribution, governance, emission calculations, inference fee payments.
technical.what_is_offchain: ML model execution, inference generation, data processing, model training, ground truth provision.
technical.audit_note: No public security audit reports found from any recognised firm. Significant gap for a network handling financial predictions.

tokenomics.critical_unlock_event: November 2026 — 48.55% of supply (Early Backers + Core Contributors) begins cliff unlock. First 33% tranche (~160M tokens) vs ~220M current circulating.
tokenomics.burn_mechanism: None documented. Excess fees accumulate in Emissions Treasury.
tokenomics.staking_apy: ~12% base, up to 50% with Allora Prime (9-month programme)

network_metrics.validators_note: 16 at mainnet launch, may have expanded
network_metrics.workers_note: Includes testnet participants, likely inflated by airdrop farming
network_metrics.revenue_note: No independently verified revenue. PWYW model allows zero-fee consumption.

infrastructure_decentralisation.evidence: Cosmos SDK L1 with CometBFT is a solid foundation. Only 16 validators at mainnet launch — highly concentrated. Workers are permissionless but 288K figure includes testnet/airdrop farmers. Consumer contracts on Arbitrum add cross-chain reach. Off-chain ML execution introduces centralisation vectors without zkML fully deployed.

governance_decentralisation.evidence: ALLO holders can vote on upgrades, emissions, and grants. But 48.55% of supply is locked to insiders. No evidence of active governance proposals or meaningful voter turnout. Foundation holds significant discretionary control.

token_distribution_fairness.evidence: Early Backers 31.05% + Core Contributors 17.50% = 48.55% insider allocation. Community Pool only 9.3%. Binance airdrop just 1.5%. Heavy insider concentration despite 3-year vesting.

censorship_resistance.evidence: Permissionless worker/reputer participation. Topics can be created by anyone. Cosmos SDK inherits reasonable censorship resistance. But 16 validators could coordinate censorship. PWYW model means topics can be economically starved.

data_sovereignty.evidence: Workers retain ML model ownership — no upload of weights required. zkML claims to verify execution without exposing proprietary weights (implementation status unclear). Architecture designed for data sovereignty by default.

open_source_transparency.evidence: Fully open source, Apache 2.0. 30+ repos, 1,127 commits. SDKs in Go, Python, TypeScript. Whitepaper public. No security audit — significant transparency gap.

token_utility.evidence: ALLO structurally required for inference purchases, topic creation, worker/reputer bonding, staking, governance. PWYW model creates demand-side uncertainty — consumers can set prices to zero. Genuine utility with revenue risk.

value_accrual.evidence: Fees flow to workers/reputers, not passive holders. No burn or buyback. Staking rewards (~12% APY) are emission-funded, not revenue-funded. Allora Prime up to 50% APY is time-limited and also emission-funded. No independent evidence of meaningful fee revenue.

supply_dynamics.evidence: Critical: 48.55% of supply cliff unlocks November 2026. First 33% tranche (~160M tokens) nearly doubles ~220M circulating supply. FDV/MCap 3.65x. Only 22% circulating. Disinflationary emissions positive long-term but near-term unlock pressure severe.

revenue_sustainability.evidence: No independently verified revenue. Not on DeFiLlama or Token Terminal. PWYW allows zero-fee consumption. 692M inferences and 55 topics self-reported, include testnet. DeFi integrations exist but revenue contribution unknown.

liquidity_access.evidence: Binance, Coinbase, Kraken, OKX, KuCoin, Gate.io, MEXC. $21.8M daily volume on $25.6M market cap — excellent volume/mcap ratio. Tier-1 exchange coverage from day one.
